originalDescription: This alert is triggered when the Autopilot Secondary does not receive a periodic message from the Autopilot Primary that sets the Drivers Assistance System (DAS) electronic control unit's (ECU) internal Ethernet switch to either allow or deny external network traffic. When connected with Toolbox to a Model S or X vehicle, the DAS ECU must allow traffic to pass to the media control unit (MCU) to enable communication with the vehicle. There might be an internal communication issue between the Autopilot Secondary and the Autopilot Primary, or the Autopilot Primary is not running as expected.

description: Tesla warning code in the Autopilot System (APS) module. This alert is triggered when the Autopilot Secondary does not receive a periodic message from the Autopilot Primary that sets the Drivers Assistance System (DAS) electronic control unit's (ECU) internal Ethernet switch to either allow or deny external network traffic. When connected with Toolbox to a Model S or X vehicle, the DAS ECU must allow traffic to pass to the media control unit (MCU) to enable communication with the vehicle. There might be an internal communication issue between the Autopilot Secondary and the Autopilot Primary, or the Autopilot Primary is not running as expected.

originalDescription: The left indicator is not functioning as expected and may be flashing faster than normal. If a fault (open, short, over-current..etc) is detected on any of the driven lamp outputs required for signaling (which will be different for each vehicle region), then the frequency of the flashing signal sent by the BCM to the turn signals is intentionally doubled in speed to indicate an issue. The vehicle can only determine if the issue is for the left or right indicator. It cannot determine if the issue is in the front or rear indicator, so they both must be checked.

originalDescription: The Battery Management System (BMS) has detected that there is a brick voltage imbalance in the high voltage (HV) battery pack. This alert was formerly named BMS_w064_SW_SOC_Imbalance in firmware versions prior to 17.20.26. If BMS_w064_SW_SOC_Imbalance appears on the vehicle or in the logs, update the vehicle firmware to the current version.

originalDescription: The high voltage (HV) battery detected an unexpected HV bus behavior. Often, this fault will trigger when the HV battery does a precharge dynamic weld check. Prior to closing the contactors, the Battery Management System (BMS) checks for any stuck or welded contactors. The BMS moves the pack plus and pack minus, after which the BMS compares movement of both packs. The BMS expects no movement from the Direct Current (DC) link plus and DC link minus.

description: Tesla fault code in the Battery Management System (BMS) module. The high voltage (HV) battery detected an unexpected HV bus behavior. originalDescription: A high voltage (HV) battery cell voltage sensor error is detected. There are three reasons for this alert to be reported by the Battery Management System (BMS): BMS_a107_errorType = BMS_A107_MODULEBRICK_MISMATCHThis alerts means that the voltage of the module differs by more than 35mV from the sum of the brick voltages of that module.BMS_a107_errorType = BMS_A107_TVSH_BLEEDV_LOWThis alert type means that the bleed resistor is turned on for that brick, but the voltage is too low (likely due to a stuck bleed resistor).BMS_a107_errorType = BMS_A107_TVSH_IMP_HIGH This alert type is the most common. The system has detected a high impedance on the sensor harness from the Battery Monitoring Board (BMB) to the bricks / module. It means that the measurements from that BMB cannot be trusted.
title: BMS_f107 - SW Cell Voltage Sensor
description: Tesla fault code in the Battery Management System (BMS) module. A high voltage (HV) battery cell voltage sensor error is detected. There are three reasons for this alert to be reported by the Battery Management System (BMS): BMS_a107_errorType = BMS_A107_MODULEBRICK_MISMATCHThis alerts means that the voltage of the module differs by more than 35mV from the sum of the brick voltages of that module.BMS_a107_errorType = BMS_A107_TVSH_BLEEDV_LOWThis alert type means that the bleed resistor is turned on for that brick, but the voltage is too low (likely due to a stuck bleed resistor).BMS_a107_errorType = BMS_A107_TVSH_IMP_HIGH This alert type is the most common. The system has detected a high impedance on the sensor harness from the Battery Monitoring Board (BMB) to the bricks / module. It means that the measurements from that BMB cannot be trusted.

originalDescription: The Battery Management System (BMS) detected that the length of time it took the vehicle to precharge was too short. During the precharge sequence when the BMS is closing the contactors, the time it takes to reach target precharge voltage is too short. If the DC link voltage rise does not match a time constant with a 35% tolerance, then a precharge fault shall be asserted and precharge will be aborted. The precharge being too short means that the voltage on the DC Link bus (vehicle side of contactors) rose faster than expected, similar to how if there was not enough capacitance on the bus.
title: BMS_f149 - SW precharge too short
description: Tesla fault code in the Battery Management System (BMS) module. The Battery Management System (BMS) detected that the length of time it took the vehicle to precharge was too short. During the precharge sequence when the BMS is closing the contactors, the time it takes to reach target precharge voltage is too short. If the DC link voltage rise does not match a time constant with a 35% tolerance, then a precharge fault shall be asserted and precharge will be aborted. The precharge being too short means that the voltage on the DC Link bus (vehicle side of contactors) rose faster than expected, similar to how if there was not enough capacitance on the bus.

originalDescription: If the contactors are trying to close and the Direct Current (DC) link voltage rise does not match a time constant with a 35% tolerance, then a precharge fault shall be asserted and precharge aborted. During the precharge sequence when the Battery Management System (BMS) is closing the contactors, the time it takes to reach target precharge voltage is too long. The precharge taking too long means that the voltage on the DC Link bus (vehicle side of contactors) rose slower than expected, similar to how if there was too much capacitance or resistance to chassis on the bus

originalDescription: The intent of this alert is to try to detect potential Battery Monitor Board (BMB) hardware (HW) issues related to the Analog to Digital Converter (ADC) Voltage Reference (Vref), which could lead to f107 Temperature Voltage Sense Harness (TVSH) faults. To detect a potential Vref HW issue, the Battery Management System (BMS) takes several "throw-away" samples from the ADC and makes sure they all match. If they do not match, there is a reference issue, and the alert is flagged.

originalDescription: "Uncorking" 75D vehicles means a couple of things: Increasing the motor torque.Increasing the maximum discharge current allowed for the high voltage (HV) battery. Some early 75kwh HV batteries were built with a shunt that had a hardware over current (HWOC) threshold which could trigger false HWOCs during a wide open throttle event due to ripple current that could cause the current to go over the uncorked current. In this case, the Battery Management System (BMS) will report the alert BMS_u024_Not_Uncorked_Capable? to let the customer and Service know that the vehicle is uncorked with a non-suitable HV battery. The current limit will not be increased, and the vehicle will not have uncorked performance.
title: BMS_u024 - Not Uncorked Capable

Some early 75kwh HV batteries were built with a shunt that had a hardware over current (HWOC) threshold which could trigger false HWOCs during a wide open throttle event due to ripple current that could cause the current to go over the uncorked current. In this case, the Battery Management System (BMS) will report the alert BMS_u024_Not_Uncorked_Capable? to let the customer and Service know that the vehicle is uncorked with a non-suitable HV battery. The current limit will not be increased, and the vehicle will not have uncorked performance.

originalDescription: This alert is for engineering visibility. It does not mean the pack has an issue, and the pack should not be replaced due to this alert. originalDescription: The addition of a Limp Reaction to Repeated Isolation (f027) alert so the customers could feel a limp response a few drive cycles before the vehicle became Vehicle Off Road (VOR) was requested to allow customers to have ample warning and a noticeable driving symptom before the VOR occurs. The current specification will set a limp when one of the following conditions are met: 2 drive cycles with alert 35 (SW_Isolation). 7 drive cycles with alert 142 (SW_Isolation_Degradatio). 2 drive cycles with alert 028 (SW_Drive_Iso_StdDev).
title: BMS_w172 - SW Drive Iso Warning
description: Tesla warning code in the Battery Management System (BMS) module. The addition of a Limp Reaction to Repeated Isolation (f027) alert so the customers could feel a limp response a few drive cycles before the vehicle became Vehicle Off Road (VOR) was requested to allow customers to have ample warning and a noticeable driving symptom before the VOR occurs. The current specification will set a limp when one of the following conditions are met: 2 drive cycles with alert 35 (SW_Isolation). 7 drive cycles with alert 142 (SW_Isolation_Degradatio). 2 drive cycles with alert 028 (SW_Drive_Iso_StdDev).

originalDescription: The charging process was interrupted by the complex programmable logic device (CPLD) due to a very sudden increase of line current above the limit threshold. The alert may be falsely set due to extreme AC line voltage disturbances from other equipment. The Gen 1 charger spec defines the alert as line peak over-current trip level shall be <= 85A with a response time < 1us. The Gen 2 charger spec defines the alert as greater than 33A (tolerance of +/- 1.7A) per phase board.

originalDescription: This fault indicates that the voltage decay after the supercharger external isolation test has happened too quickly. During supercharger startup, the supercharger will open the output contactors after performing the external isolation test at 300V. The vehicle then monitors the time it takes for the 300V to decay. If the decay time is sufficient, the start-up sequence proceeds. The purpose of this test is to check the common mode isolation (DC+ to DC-) of the high voltage chain from the supercharger cabinet to the vehicle's fast charge contactors.

If the decay time is sufficient, the start-up sequence proceeds. The purpose of this test is to check the common mode isolation (DC+ to DC-) of the high voltage chain from the supercharger cabinet to the vehicle's fast charge contactors.

originalDescription: The line impedance is too high, or the line voltage is dropping significantly from another source drawing current on the same circuit. If this alert is present, the voltage, which is monitored continuously during charging, has dropped by 14% or more for a period longer than 1 second from the initial voltage sample value that was taken at the beginning of charge before current started flowing and the vehicle will not be able to charge.

originalDescription: This alert is currently used for GB (Guobiao standard - Chinese) charge ports on Model S / X vehicles and PLCRLY electronic control units (ECU) in EU Model S / X vehicles. The alert means the proximity voltage is outside of expected range. These are the expected ranges on vehicles with a GB charge port, the AC portion of the charge port, and the IEC type 2 charge ports: 4.291V to 4.620V - No cable in the GB AC charge port. 4.280V to 4.291V - GB cable in the GB AC charge port, and the button is being held down, meaning the connector is unlatched.3.525V to 3.892V - 10A GB cable in the GB AC charge port. 2.995V to 3.275V - 16A GB cable in the GB AC charge port.1.740V to 2.035V - 32A GB cable in the GB AC charge port.1.020V to 1.220V - 63A GB cable in the GB AC charge port. These are the limits for the DC portion of the GB charge port: For pin CC2: 4.5V to 5.5V - No cable in the GB DC charge port.2.0V to 3.0V - Cable connected to the GB DC charge port.For pin CC1:0.0V to 0.5V - No cable in the GB DC charge port.5.5V to 6.5V - Cable connected to the GB DC charge port.3.5V to 4.5V - Cable latched to the GB DC charge port. Any voltage outside any of these voltage regions will cause a proximityRationality alert and prevent charging.

description: Tesla warning code in the Charge Port (CP) module. This alert is currently used for GB (Guobiao standard - Chinese) charge ports on Model S / X vehicles and PLCRLY electronic control units (ECU) in EU Model S / X vehicles. The alert means the proximity voltage is outside of expected range. These are the expected ranges on vehicles with a GB charge port, the AC portion of the charge port, and the IEC type 2 charge ports: 4.291V to 4.620V - No cable in the GB AC charge port. 4.280V to 4.291V - GB cable in the GB AC charge port, and the button is being held down, meaning the connector is unlatched.3.525V to 3.892V - 10A GB cable in the GB AC charge port. 2.995V to 3.275V - 16A GB cable in the GB AC charge port.1.740V to 2.035V - 32A GB cable in the GB AC charge port.1.020V to 1.220V - 63A GB cable in the GB AC charge port. These are the limits for the DC portion of the GB charge port: For pin CC2: 4.5V to 5.5V - No cable in the GB DC charge port.2.0V to 3.0V - Cable connected to the GB DC charge port.For pin CC1:0.0V to 0.5V - No cable in the GB DC charge port.5.5V to 6.5V - Cable connected to the GB DC charge port.3.5V to 4.5V - Cable latched to the GB DC charge port. Any voltage outside any of these voltage regions will cause a proximityRationality alert and prevent charging.

originalDescription: This particular fault is quite heavily debounced in the Charge Port (CP). It only sets when the following conditions have been met for over 3 seconds:Door sensed as closed. Valid pilot (LINE_CHARGE, FAST_CHARGE, or FAULTED) or proximity (LATCHED or UNLATCHED) is present. If there is an issue with the door sensor that indicates the position as closed when it is opened, this alert may be triggered. Also, the alert can be triggered if the pilot or proximity state reported by the VI was stale / delayed.
title: CP_w029 - door Closed Prox Pilot
description: Tesla warning code in the Charge Port (CP) module. This particular fault is quite heavily debounced in the Charge Port (CP). If there is an issue with the door sensor that indicates the position as closed when it is opened, this alert may be triggered. Also, the alert can be triggered if the pilot or proximity state reported by the VI was stale / delayed.

If the Charge Port Door and Latch has been calibrated properly, the alert should clear once the door is returned to the closed position.

The alert triggers when the Charge Port Door is manually forced open when it is expected to be closed. If the Charge Port Door and Latch has been calibrated properly, the alert should clear once the door is returned to the closed position.

originalDescription: The latch has moved into a position that prevents charging. If this alert is present, the latch may have been inadvertently moved manually or when a charge attempt times out and the latch locks into its default position.

originalDescription: This alert sets when the latch is being driven open and the measured latch position is not in the DISENGAGED state for over 600ms. The alert clears when the latch returns to the DISENGAGED state or the latch is no longer being driven open.
title: CP_w036 - latch Not Disengaged
description: Tesla warning code in the Charge Port (CP) module. This alert sets when the latch is being driven open and the measured latch position is not in the DISENGAGED state for over 600ms. The alert clears when the latch returns to the DISENGAGED state or the latch is no longer being driven open.

originalDescription: The alert sets when the latch is not driven and the measured latch position is not in the ENGAGED state. It clears when the latch returns to the ENGAGED state or the latch is driven open.
title: CP_w037 - latch Not Engaged
description: Tesla warning code in the Charge Port (CP) module. The alert sets when the latch is not driven and the measured latch position is not in the ENGAGED state. It clears when the latch returns to the ENGAGED state or the latch is driven open.

originalDescription: This alert sets when the latch is not driven and the measured latch position is not in the BLOCKING state. It clears when the latch returns to the BLOCKING state or the latch is driven open.
title: CP_w038 - latch Not Blocking
description: Tesla warning code in the Charge Port (CP) module. This alert sets when the latch is not driven and the measured latch position is not in the BLOCKING state. It clears when the latch returns to the BLOCKING state or the latch is driven open.

originalDescription: If this alert triggers while a customer is attempting to charge, gently inquire: Did the customer wait several minutes between opening the Charge Port and inserting the charging cable?If yes, this can cause the latch to move back into its default position and block the charge cable insert.Did the customer use a finger or tool to move the latch out of the way to insert the charge cable?If yes, this is what triggered the alert. Did the customer unscrew the charge port cover and move the latch?If yes, advise the customer that this is dangerous and please bring the vehicle in for a inspection. Did the customer use the manual release lever in the trunk to release the charge port latch and then attempt to charge?If yes, this is what triggered the alert.
title: CP_w039 - latch Movement 2
description: Tesla warning code in the Charge Port (CP) module. If this alert triggers while a customer is attempting to charge, gently inquire: Did the customer wait several minutes between opening the Charge Port and inserting the charging cable?If yes, this can cause the latch to move back into its default position and block the charge cable insert.Did the customer use a finger or tool to move the latch out of the way to insert the charge cable?If yes, this is what triggered the alert. Did the customer unscrew the charge port cover and move the latch?If yes, advise the customer that this is dangerous and please bring the vehicle in for a inspection. Did the customer use the manual release lever in the trunk to release the charge port latch and then attempt to charge?If yes, this is what triggered the alert.

originalDescription: Drive Inverter Warning: Inputs to stability control have degraded The actuators required for stability control are in degraded states, so stability control is also degraded. Check tractionControlState and espActuatorState to find the specific controller that is degrading stability control. For tractionControlState, check previous DI alerts that point to specific cause of their degredation. For espActuatorState, check for ESP alerts that point to the specific cause of its degredation. This alert will clear on the drive cycle after the root cause has been corrected. Traction control and stability control may fault due to this error. The vehicle should be driven with extreme caution if these sytems are faulted. originalDescription: Drive Inverter Warning: Traction control (TC) is disabled This alert sets any time traction control is not available. This is almost always due to the system being faulted. To diagnose the cause of the fault, check this alert's payload to identify which inputs are in a degraded state. Steps to find cause: 1) If velocityEstimatorState is degraded, check other DI alert for velocityEstimatorState to find the root cause. If velocityEstimatorState is normal: 2) If btcTargetState or ptcTargetState are degraded, check for CAN bus issues (these signals come from ESP) or sensor issues with ESP (check associated ESP alerts). If target states are normal: 3) If addState is degraded, check for other DI alerts about ADD. If slaveADDState is degraded, check for DIS alerts about ADD and check for other DI alerts indicating CAN communication issues with DIS. This alert will clear on the drive cycle after traction control is re-enabled or un-faulted. Traction control is not available. The vehicle should be driven with extreme caution. This is almost always due to the system being faulted. To diagnose the cause of the fault, check DI alerts set immediately prior to this alert. Also ensure the vehicle is on consistent software with no software version mismatch alerts. This alert will clear on the drive cycle after stability control is re-enabled or un-faulted. originalDescription: A brake pedal sensor problem detected. This alert has been setting due to either the brake pedal switch not being installed properly or malfunctioning switches. The switch is a two part switch where one portion of the switch sends brake signals to the Gateway, electronic stability program (ESP) module, and the rear drive unit, while the other brake signal is sent directly to the rear drive unit for regeneration purposes. When the brake signal at the rear drive unit is not the same as the signal at the ESP module, this alert is triggered.

The switch is a two part switch where one portion of the switch sends brake signals to the Gateway, electronic stability program (ESP) module, and the rear drive unit, while the other brake signal is sent directly to the rear drive unit for regeneration purposes. When the brake signal at the rear drive unit is not the same as the signal at the ESP module, this alert is triggered.

originalDescription: The brake pedal is required before shifting into gear. If the vehicle has been in neutral for more than 1 second and the vehicle speed is less than 0.8kph (0.5mph), the brake must be pressed in order to enter Drive or Reverse.
title: DI_u001 - brake Shift Req
description: Tesla unknown code in the Drive Inverter (DI) module. The brake pedal is required before shifting into gear. If the vehicle has been in neutral for more than 1 second and the vehicle speed is less than 0.8kph (0.5mph), the brake must be pressed in order to enter Drive or Reverse.

originalDescription: The drive inverter has prevented the shifting between gear selections based on one of the following criteria not being met:If the vehicle is shifting from Drive to Reverse or Reverse to Drive, the vehicle's speed must be lower than 5mph. If the vehicle is shifting from Drive or Reverse or Neutral to Park, the vehicle's speed must be lower than 1.4mph in any direction. If the vehicle is shifting from Park or Neutral to Drive or Reverse, the vehicle has been in P or N for more than 1 second, and the vehicle's speed is less than 5mph, the accelerator must be engaged at less than 5% torque.
title: DI_u002 - shift Denied
description: Tesla unknown code in the Drive Inverter (DI) module. The drive inverter has prevented the shifting between gear selections based on one of the following criteria not being met:If the vehicle is shifting from Drive to Reverse or Reverse to Drive, the vehicle's speed must be lower than 5mph. If the vehicle is shifting from Drive or Reverse or Neutral to Park, the vehicle's speed must be lower than 1.4mph in any direction. If the vehicle is shifting from Park or Neutral to Drive or Reverse, the vehicle has been in P or N for more than 1 second, and the vehicle's speed is less than 5mph, the accelerator must be engaged at less than 5% torque.

originalDescription: This alert indicates that the tire and wheel setup has changed from a square (equal diameter) set to a staggered (different diameters) set or vice-versa. For example, if 19" wheels are installed on a P85+, this alert will be triggered. Currently (2.2.140), it is not shown to the customer, but that may change when more data has been collected. The alert contains information about what the fitment was changed to. The alert is not set if the module reasserts the same tire fitment or when the wear factors get reset to default for other reasons, such as EEPROM corruption, etc. The alert shall only be set once per key cycle. This is controlled by the tire detection algorithm, not the alert specific code. The silent user alert will not clear, with exception of a power cycle, meaning there is no clearing logic in the code.
title: DI_u017 - tire Fitment Changed
description: Tesla unknown code in the Drive Inverter (DI) module. This alert indicates that the tire and wheel setup has changed from a square (equal diameter) set to a staggered (different diameters) set or vice-versa. For example, if 19" wheels are installed on a P85+, this alert will be triggered. Currently (2.2.140), it is not shown to the customer, but that may change when more data has been collected. The alert contains information about what the fitment was changed to. The alert is not set if the module reasserts the same tire fitment or when the wear factors get reset to default for other reasons, such as EEPROM corruption, etc. The alert shall only be set once per key cycle. This is controlled by the tire detection algorithm, not the alert specific code. The silent user alert will not clear, with exception of a power cycle, meaning there is no clearing logic in the code.

originalDescription: This alert detects when active damping is producing a torque signal that is higher than expected for longer than expected. Active damping compensates for half the shaft / wheel dynamics to reduce drivetrain oscillation. It also tries to mitigate the ?clunk? that comes with a torque direction reversal. When this alert is thrown, the active damping is turned off but turns back on (retries) after 2 seconds. After 2 retries, it stays off until the end of the drive?.
title: DI_w014 - active Damping
description: Tesla warning code in the Drive Inverter (DI) module. This alert detects when active damping is producing a torque signal that is higher than expected for longer than expected. Active damping compensates for half the shaft / wheel dynamics to reduce drivetrain oscillation. It also tries to mitigate the ?clunk? that comes with a torque direction reversal. When this alert is thrown, the active damping is turned off but turns back on (retries) after 2 seconds. After 2 retries, it stays off until the end of the drive?.

originalDescription: A brake pedal sensor problem detected. This alert has been setting due to either the brake pedal switch not being installed properly or defective switches. The switch is a two part switch where one portion of the switch sends brake signals to the Gateway, electronic stability program (ESP) module, and the rear drive unit while the other brake signal is sent directly to the rear drive unit for regeneration purposes. When the brake signal at the rear drive unit is not the same as the signal at the ESP module, this alert is triggered.

originalDescription: The Drive Unit has detected an issue with the cruise feature. If this alert is present, there may have been an invalid brake switch signal when the cruise feature is Enabled or is in Standby. There may be an issue with the Controller Area Network (CAN) bus communication components related to the cruise feature, and there may also be alerts related to the motor encoder, the pedal monitor, and / or the CAN bus.
title: DI_w124 - cruise Fault
description: Tesla warning code in the Drive Inverter (DI) module. The Drive Unit has detected an issue with the cruise feature. If this alert is present, there may have been an invalid brake switch signal when the cruise feature is Enabled or is in Standby. There may be an issue with the Controller Area Network (CAN) bus communication components related to the cruise feature, and there may also be alerts related to the motor encoder, the pedal monitor, and / or the CAN bus.

originalDescription: The Drive Unit has detected a high voltage (HV) battery low power condition.
title: DI_w125 - no Battery Power

originalDescription: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected that the vehicle has entered Limp mode.

originalDescription: The Drive Inverter has detected that the vehicle has begun a Graceful Power Off. The motor controller will assert this condition whenever any of the following alerts or controller area network (CAN) signals are present: No Battery Power (any amount of time)GTW_notEnough12VforDrive (any amount of time) This alert will also trigger whenever any of the temperatures below exceed their Graceful Power Off thresholds for the specified time:Outlet fluid (2 seconds)Internal ambient (2 seconds)Any of the Isolated Gate Bipolar Transistor (IGBT) heatsinks (3 seconds)Stator (2 seconds)Fluid delta T too positive (2 seconds)
title: DI_w127 - graceful Power Off
description: Tesla warning code in the Drive Inverter (DI) module. The Drive Inverter has detected that the vehicle has begun a Graceful Power Off. The motor controller will assert this condition whenever any of the following alerts or controller area network (CAN) signals are present: No Battery Power (any amount of time)GTW_notEnough12VforDrive (any amount of time) This alert will also trigger whenever any of the temperatures below exceed their Graceful Power Off thresholds for the specified time:Outlet fluid (2 seconds)Internal ambient (2 seconds)Any of the Isolated Gate Bipolar Transistor (IGBT) heatsinks (3 seconds)Stator (2 seconds)Fluid delta T too positive (2 seconds)

originalDescription: The drive inverter (DI) has detected that the high voltage interlock loop (HVIL) voltage is outside of the specified range. This alert only applies to small drive units. This alert is set if HVIL voltage is +/-0.3 volts or +/- 1.5ma the nominal value. This alert can be falsely triggered when the DI is outputting higher torque. This alert alone is not enough to identify an HVIL issue.

description: Tesla warning code in the Drive Inverter (DI) module. The drive inverter (DI) has detected that the high voltage interlock loop (HVIL) voltage is outside of the specified range. This alert only applies to small drive units. This alert is set if HVIL voltage is +/-0.3 volts or +/- 1.5ma the nominal value. This alert can be falsely triggered when the DI is outputting higher torque. This alert alone is not enough to identify an HVIL issue.

originalDescription: This alert was formerly named DIS_f147_overtorqueUnavailable in firmware versions prior to 2017.6 (17.6.15). If DIS_f147_overtorqueUnavailable appears on the vehicle or in the logs, update the vehicle firmware to the current version. The algorithm behind this alert has evolved over time and may continue to evolve. While DIS_f147_overtorqueUnavailable actively limited torque levels to prevent Drive Unit malfunction, the new alert, DIS_f147_highTorqueWearLimit, reminds customers to take it easy but does not actively limit torque levels.

originalDescription: The external 12V supply voltage from the vehicle is out of range. This alert sets when the measured voltage is detected to be below 7.31V, which indicates a power loss event. The alert is not set during an electronic control unit (ECU) reset. Once it is set, the alert is cleared when the voltage detected is above 7.31V. This alert is usually accompanied with other 12V alerts, indicating a generic 12V under voltage or sudden power loss.
title: EFUSE_w010 - Ext Supply Volt Error
description: Tesla warning code in the Electronic Fuse (EFUSE) module. The external 12V supply voltage from the vehicle is out of range. This alert sets when the measured voltage is detected to be below 7.31V, which indicates a power loss event. The alert is not set during an electronic control unit (ECU) reset. Once it is set, the alert is cleared when the voltage detected is above 7.31V. This alert is usually accompanied with other 12V alerts, indicating a generic 12V under voltage or sudden power loss.

originalDescription: This alert will trigger when the vbat-fused high current E-fuse upstream of the air compressor E-fuse internal to the E-fuse module is faulted as seen by the E-fuse electronic control unit (ECU). originalDescription: The power steering can only be disabled due to the presence of "E-fuse self-tests" upon vehicle wake-up. If either the Electric Power Assist Steering (EPAS) primary or EPAS secondary fault at wake-up, there will be no assistance from the start. Sleeping and waking the vehicle will force a re-test, which will pass the next time if the conditions that caused the initial failure are not present. If the failure occurred during the drive itself, it is less likely to be E-fuse related. Once driving is underway with a working EPAS, the E-fuse software state is locked out for safety reasons and only the hardware sensing can make it trip. Both the EPAS circuits would have needed to trip their E-fuse during the journey for this to be the case.?

originalDescription: Motor Driver Runtime Error: Calculation for new Pulse Width Modulation (PWM) commands has timed out. FOR has missed the time at which the PWM values for the next 500?s cycle must be calculated. The output of the PWM is cyclical, meaning one cycle is divided into several sections. After about 75% of the cycle time, the FOR needs the PWM values for the next cycle to be calculated. After the completion of processing the measured values started by the FOR, it must compute the new PWM values in approximately 20% of the cycle time. If the FOR takes longer than the beginning of the new cycle, the Error Handler reports an error and then recalculation begins.

originalDescription: The core self-test (Library from NEC) has failed. Once developed by the processor, the test routine is run. The registry hardware and machine instructions are called. If the return value of the test routine from the fixed value occurs, the fault entry triggers.

originalDescription: This alert indicates there is a phase comparison error with the motor driver. Upon detection of a phase short-circuit (either against each other or between the ground and UBATT), it is transferred to a safe state after 100ms. There is an interruption to the servomotor. This monitoring is active. The FOR and rotor speed must also be active for less than 500rpm, and the nominal current over an applicable value lie. If the conditions listed are satisfied and the actual current differs by more than 50% of the nominal current, the components' internal error filtering begins, which happens 100ms after reporting an error to the Error Handler. If it is within the error filter time (100ms) and the deviation is less than 50%, the components' internal back-filtering half-gradient of the error filtering is enabled.

title: EPAS3P_d071 - veh Spd Corr Error
description: Tesla diagnostic code in the Electric Power Steering Primary (EPAS3P) module. This alert triggers when the electronic stability program (ESP) vehicle speed does not match the drive inverter (DI) vehicle speed. Traction / stability control interventions can occasionally trigger false positives.

originalDescription: The feedforward is a value which is an output of the control of the motor. It allows it to anticipate a correction and overall react faster. This alert says that the error is greater than expected, which could mean that the steering motor can't successfully reach its target as what is measured is too much off the expected value.
title: EPAS3P_d072 - mot Feed Fwd Error
description: Tesla diagnostic code in the Electric Power Steering Primary (EPAS3P) module. The feedforward is a value which is an output of the control of the motor. It allows it to anticipate a correction and overall react faster. This alert says that the error is greater than expected, which could mean that the steering motor can't successfully reach its target as what is measured is too much off the expected value.

originalDescription: This alert is expected to set every time during power-up in some parts. The reason this alert sets is because hardware-specific motor parameters are outside of the range expected by the firmware. originalDescription: This monitoring detects a wrong sign of steering angle signal. The yaw rate calculated from steering angle and the yaw rate calculated from the wheel speed sensors are compared. If the measured yaw rate fits to the model yaw rate positive integrals (IVzLwVA and IVzLwHA) are calculated but if the negative values fits the integrals will become negative. A fault is determined if the integral value is negative and forward driving is recognized. This fault will cause a release test in the following ignition cycle to assure that the sensor is build in correctly. This fault can also be caused by swapped WSS lines at one axle. It requires a release check in the following ignition cycle to prevent system release when the fault is still present. The conditions for release is positive integrals (IVzLwVA and IVzLwHA) > +180 deg. UI_odo message integrity check failed ESP is providing hydraulic boost due to power brakes failure ESP has lost communication with the iBooster, or iBooster has lost the ability to support brake boost Check iBooster status and traffic on the PARTY and CHASSIS bus Brake boost functionality degraded Yawrate signal received from RCM exceeds message latency specification

description: Tesla alert code in the Electronic Stability Program (ESP) module. This monitoring detects a wrong sign of steering angle signal. The yaw rate calculated from steering angle and the yaw rate calculated from the wheel speed sensors are compared. If the measured yaw rate fits to the model yaw rate positive integrals (IVzLwVA and IVzLwHA) are calculated but if the negative values fits the integrals will become negative. A fault is determined if the integral value is negative and forward driving is recognized. This fault will cause a release test in the following ignition cycle to assure that the sensor is build in correctly. This fault can also be caused by swapped WSS lines at one axle. It requires a release check in the following ignition cycle to prevent system release when the fault is still present. The conditions for release is positive integrals (IVzLwVA and IVzLwHA) > +180 deg. originalDescription: Invalid Brake Pedal Status received from CAN(Signal: BrakePedalApplied_Q) iBST_brakeAppliedQF is invalid Invalid Data Received From Drive Motor Control Module B: Data Length Code, Checksum or Parity invalid Steering Wheel Position Sensor: Plausibility fault The monitoring checks for a physically plausible steering angle signal and consists of following parts:- Sas range monitoringReceived raw sensor hardware value is higher than the maximum, physical steering wheel turn from zero-position to lock in the vehicle (LwAnschlMax) + an additional robustness tolerance value (typical 90 deg)- Sas gradient monitoringIf the signal gradient (steering angle velocity) from one 20 ms-cycle to another is higher than 40 deg for 2 SAS Messages (60 deg for more than 2 SAS messages or no SAS message counter) or - change of this gradient (steering angle acceleration) is higher than 30 deg a failure will be set.- Sas plausibility versus yaw rateThe difference between measured steering angle signal and model value calculated from yaw rate signal is evaluated for fault detection. Dependent on the driving conditions failures in size of (10 + 60 [m/s] / velocity [m/s]) deg at steering angle are recognized through three possible recognition paths evaluating stable driving conditions. This is to prevent a fault recognition due to unstable driving.When the difference is caused by a fault of the steering angle sensor a steering sensor angle fault will be entered. The sensor - yaw rate sensor(YRS) or steering angle sensor(SAS) - showing the largest deviation to the reference yaw rate is marked as faulty. ESP detected vehicle skidding with locked wheels Internal electronic fault detected on USV valves. The unit has detected an internal fault in the USV valves or valve controls Invalid Data Received FromBrake Booster iBooster : Invalid Signal Status. IBST_pRunout is invalid on CAN

originalDescription: Invalid Data Received From Drive Motor Control Module A: Alive Counter invalid Wheel Speed Sensor: General Fault. If the difference between maximum and minimum wheel speed related to a reference speed exceeds 5%. A generic wheel speed sensor fault is set if no wheel specific fault could be determined. During system mode ABS and backup-EBD (Electr. Brake Force Distribution) and below 20 kilometers per hour the deviation is 6 kilometers per hour for velocities 100 kilometers per hour. 1.) Open circuit in the WSS line 2.) Short circuit to + voltage in the WSS line 3.) Short circuit to GND in WSS line 4.) WSS dislodging from bracket 5.) Input amplifier in ECU faulty 6.) tooth wheel missing 7.) WSS not installed 8.) too great airgap 9.) incorrect tooth count Invalid Data Received From Pedal Monitor: Alive Counter invalid The ESP is back in a normal ABS state

title: ESP_d057 - ays Mon Range
description: Tesla diagnostic code in the Electronic Stability Program (ESP) module. Lateral Acceleration Sensor: Signal out of Range. There is an Ays failure due to the lateral acceleration being out of range, such as if the absolute value of the lateral acceleration is higher than P_ibea_AyAnschlMax (default value is 15 m/s�).

originalDescription: Lateral Acceleration Sensor: Adjustment is not possible, and the offset is too large. There is an Ays failure due to a lateral acceleration offset or mounting position that is out of limit. During normal driving, a symmetrical driving situation is assumed. In this case, the mean driving direction is straight ahead and the long time filtered lateral acceleration value is equivalent to the offset. The long time filter is a low pass filter that after 30 km, 95% of the initial offset is compensated. The offset is initialized with the Electrically Erasable Programmable Read-Only Memory (EEPROM) value (offset of the last ignition cycle). The read value is limited to 2 m/s�. A failure is set immediately when offset AysOffset exceeds the limit P_ayInOfsFineErr (default value: 2.25 m/s�). In addition, there is a check for a sudden change in the signal offset due to, for example, a misalignment of the HU after an accident. It compares the stored offset versus the new offset after a learning distance of 10 km. The allowed misalignment is 10deg (equals a 1.73 m/s� offset difference).
title: ESP_d058 - ays Offset
description: Tesla diagnostic code in the Electronic Stability Program (ESP) module. Lateral Acceleration Sensor: Adjustment is not possible, and the offset is too large. There is an Ays failure due to a lateral acceleration offset or mounting position that is out of limit. During normal driving, a symmetrical driving situation is assumed. In this case, the mean driving direction is straight ahead and the long time filtered lateral acceleration value is equivalent to the offset. The long time filter is a low pass filter that after 30 km, 95% of the initial offset is compensated. The offset is initialized with the Electrically Erasable Programmable Read-Only Memory (EEPROM) value (offset of the last ignition cycle). The read value is limited to 2 m/s�. A failure is set immediately when offset AysOffset exceeds the limit P_ayInOfsFineErr (default value: 2.25 m/s�). In addition, there is a check for a sudden change in the signal offset due to, for example, a misalignment of the HU after an accident. It compares the stored offset versus the new offset after a learning distance of 10 km. The allowed misalignment is 10deg (equals a 1.73 m/s� offset difference).

originalDescription: Lateral Acceleration Sensor: Plausibility against second signal failed. Ays plausibility fault. The monitoring checks for a physically plausible lateral acceleration signal and consists of the following parts: Monitoring during validity by building a reference lateral acceleration from all available sensor signals.Steering angle.Yaw rate.Lateral acceleration.Wheel speed sensors. It is possible to test the lateral acceleration (Ay) signal on plausibility. If during stable vehicle behavior a lateral acceleration failure larger than approximately 2.5m/s� occurs, the vehicle dynamic controller will disregard the sensor information so a false vehicle dynamic intervention is prevented. A fault is recognized after 1.6s during model validity. When observing, the recognition time depends on the amount of failure. The deviation of the measured signal from the model values is integrated when exceeding the allowed threshold. The shutdown threshold for the fault integral is 10m/s.
title: ESP_d059 - ays Plaus
description: Tesla diagnostic code in the Electronic Stability Program (ESP) module. Lateral Acceleration Sensor: Plausibility against second signal failed. Ays plausibility fault. The monitoring checks for a physically plausible lateral acceleration signal and consists of the following parts: Monitoring during validity by building a reference lateral acceleration from all available sensor signals.Steering angle.Yaw rate.Lateral acceleration.Wheel speed sensors. It is possible to test the lateral acceleration (Ay) signal on plausibility. If during stable vehicle behavior a lateral acceleration failure larger than approximately 2.5m/s� occurs, the vehicle dynamic controller will disregard the sensor information so a false vehicle dynamic intervention is prevented. A fault is recognized after 1.6s during model validity. When observing, the recognition time depends on the amount of failure. The deviation of the measured signal from the model values is integrated when exceeding the allowed threshold. The shutdown threshold for the fault integral is 10m/s.

originalDescription: The customer has attempted to start the vehicle, and the media control unit (MCU) or instrument cluster (IC) is not fully up yet. The alert is usually present when the IC is still booting after wake-up, but it can also appear if there are issues with the audio starting on the center display. This alert will prevent the drive rail from turning on for 30 seconds. After 30 seconds, the vehicle will turn on the drive rail eventually, even without the IC or MCU being ready.

originalDescription: The 12V battery voltage has dropped below 10.5V for at least 5 seconds. If this alert is present, the 12V battery has likely become damaged through excessive discharge and should be replaced. The root cause of this damage should also be diagnosed and repaired to prevent future occurrences in the new battery. The flooded batteries used on Model X vehicles are especially sensitive to deep discharge, which can result in failures developing weeks or months later even if the battery looks fine initially.

originalDescription: The Continental Tire Pressure Monitoring System (TPMS) electronic control unit (ECU) uses an End-of-Line (EOL) routine to check the functionality of the system and the sensors. This is triggered in Service when Toolbox 2.0 is used to program Continental TPMS. During this process, Toolbox sends the sensor IDs to the ECU along with the proper recommended cold pressure (RCP) values so the ECU can calculate the thresholds for the vehicle configuration. When the Toolbox routine is complete, the ECU enters EOL mode and this alert appears. A test drive is needed in order for the ECU to learn the sensor IDs and compare them to what was entered using Toolbox. The EOL routine will only finish if the sensor IDs entered into Toolbox match the sensor IDs that the ECU reads during the test drive. ?For this reason, it is critical that the tool is reading properly, and possible causes should be explored if the ATEQ tool is not reading properly.

When the Toolbox routine is complete, the ECU enters EOL mode and this alert appears. A test drive is needed in order for the ECU to learn the sensor IDs and compare them to what was entered using Toolbox. The EOL routine will only finish if the sensor IDs entered into Toolbox match the sensor IDs that the ECU reads during the test drive. ?For this reason, it is critical that the tool is reading properly, and possible causes should be explored if the ATEQ tool is not reading properly.

originalDescription: The battery management system (BMS) constantly measures or calculates the resistance across the pack contactors by looking at the voltage difference when the current is stable between the battery side and vehicle side of the power switches. The current is always fluctuating at the power switches, meaning the measurement is never perfect, and the BMS associates a confidence index to each measurement. The more stable the current and the longer it is stable, the higher the confidence index will be. ? The Gateway will report this alert and start a log upload when: The confidence index is on the lower side (but still acceptable) and the power switch (either positive or negative) resistance measurement is above 1.5mohms. The confidence index is good (more confidence in the measurement) and the power switch (either positive or negative) resistance measurement is above 1.0mohms. In parallel, the BMS also calculates the contactors stress, which directly relates to the contactors impedance. There cannot be stress without impedance.
title: GTW_w319 - contactor Impedance
description: Tesla warning code in the Gateway (GTW) module. The battery management system (BMS) constantly measures or calculates the resistance across the pack contactors by looking at the voltage difference when the current is stable between the battery side and vehicle side of the power switches. The current is always fluctuating at the power switches, meaning the measurement is never perfect, and the BMS associates a confidence index to each measurement. The more stable the current and the longer it is stable, the higher the confidence index will be. ? The Gateway will report this alert and start a log upload when: The confidence index is on the lower side (but still acceptable) and the power switch (either positive or negative) resistance measurement is above 1.5mohms. The confidence index is good (more confidence in the measurement) and the power switch (either positive or negative) resistance measurement is above 1.0mohms. In parallel, the BMS also calculates the contactors stress, which directly relates to the contactors impedance. There cannot be stress without impedance.

originalDescription: This alert will appear if the status of the front trunk switch goes into an ambiguous state. Ambiguous state for the switch means the body controller (BCM - Model S and BCFRONT - Model X) sent a valid command to the front trunk latch but did not detected that the front trunk latch switch transitioned to OPEN state. A valid command means all preconditions were OK, and the module applied power to the latch. ??If this alert is present, Cruise Control will be disabled on Drivers Assistance System (DAS) vehicles (article 20365). The alert will clear itself after a successful release cycle: if the primary latch switch transits from CLOSED to OPEN state within 200ms following a release request.

originalDescription: The Electronic Stability Program (ESP) pump increases hydraulic pressure if the pad friction is compromised (brake fade, ice buildup, etc.) The Hydraulic Fade Compensation (HFC) is designed to help generate brake force in conditions where the brake effectiveness may be reduced due to abnormal environmental circumstances. Traditionally, this function was used for the case of overheating during steep mountain descents. However, Tesla has refined the concept for use in cold and wet conditions. Under extreme weather conditions where the brake rotors and pads may be subject to excess water, a water film on the brake surfaces could momentarily decrease the performance of the brakes. Road salts that are used in some winter climates to prevent ice formation can have a mild lubricant effect that may further degrade brake performance. In such conditions, the HFC monitors the brake system pressure and ABS-activity to identify instances of low brake performance. Additionally, brake force can be generated by running the hydraulic pump used for stability control functions. If the HFC is triggered during heavy braking, the driver may feel a slight drop in brake pedal position, ABS-like pulsation through the brake pedal, and a surge in vehicle braking. This is normal and indicates the HFC-function is activated. The Amber ABS-active light will also flash during HFC operation. ? Customer Facing Impact: If the brake effectiveness is reduced and the brake pedal is pushed hard enough to trigger HFC, the customer will feel lower braking performance until the HFC system is activated at which point they will feel a significant increase in braking power and reduction in vehicle speed.Customers will also feel the brake pedal pulled away from their foot??. This alert can be expected under certain environmental conditions as described in owners manual under the topic "Hydraulic Fade Compensation." If these environmental conditions are not met or brake performance does not regain to normal operation, pursue possible causes to investigate further.

originalDescription: The iBooster assertion has failed according the ANSI C standard. When an assertion fails the following occurs: The failure is stored in the electrically erasable programmable ROM (EEPROM) (failure memory).The program flow is aborted, and the electronic control unit (ECU) is shutdown electrically (only in the power cycle when the assertion failed). In the next power cycle, the already failed assertion is detected, and the system is shutdown hydraulically to read the failure memory. Assertions are only active if NDEBUG is not set by the compiler (see also ANSI C standard).
title: IBST_d160 - assertion Fail
description: Tesla diagnostic code in the Integrated Brake System (IBST) module. The iBooster assertion has failed according the ANSI C standard. The humidity calibration is out of range The seatback self capacitance sensor is open The frequency reading is > 45kHz The frequency reading returns below 45kHz The cushion self capacitance sensor reading has a low duty cycle The sandwich sensor is shorted to ground The cushion self capacitance sensor is not calibrated The cushion self capacitance sensor is open

originalDescription: The temperature or humidity measurement is invalid Communication with the temp/humidity sensor has been lost Communication with temp/humidity sensor is restored The sandwich sensor is not calibrated The seatback self capacitance sensor is low The passenger airbag installed is not allowed with an OCS with no sandwich The OCS determines that an incorrect airbag is installed The passenger airbag is replaced to match the expectation of OCS The cushion self capacitance sensor is shorted to ground Adjacent sensor noise detected. Set when the sensor internal temperature is over 150deg C. Cleared when sensor internal temperature is below the hysteresis level 10degc below set point Sensor function will be impaired, park assist features will be disabled. Invalid communication from SCCM At least 3 consequitive invalid messages (of same message type). Clears automatically when messages are received Some park assist functionality not availble Overvoltage detected by the ECU Sets when the ECU supply voltage is > 16V for 10 seconds or more. Resets when when the ECU supply voltage is 9.5V for 10 seconds or more. Failed to read or write NVRAM. Failure to read or write NVRAM. Clears on next normal boot cycle where read is succesful Default calibration parameters are used. Invalid communication from EPB Invalid communication from EPAS Expected messages from DAS/APP missing. Messages missing for more than 3 nominal cycle times. Sensor internal failure occured - like sensor damping resistor error or broken or disconnected membrane error, self diagnosis error or sensor reset or sensor resonator drift Expected messages from RCM missing. Sensor supply voltage outside of specificaiton Park assist functionality unavailable. Expected messages from DI missing. Expected messages from ESP missing. ECU recovered from a SW watchdog reset. ECU was previously reset due to watchdog event. Clears on next normal boot cycle The secondary controller area network (CAN) bus is in the busoff state. More than 255 consecutive messages must be missing from the secondary CAN bus. Messages are received on the secondary CAN bus. No expected impact on vehicle functionality. Invalid communication from VCFRONT Invalid communication from DAS/APP Expected messages from EPAS missing. Invalid communication from ESP CRC of the calibration block in D-Flash mismatched. On boot, the CRC of the calibration block does not match. Requires a 12v reset and the fault condition to be cleared The default calibration is used. Expected messages from EPB missing. The calibration block in D-Flash is missing or not available On boot, the calibration block cannot be read. Expected messages from SCCM missing. Hardware watchdog not pet within a specified time. Clears automatically as ECU will reset Park assist functionality not availble Expected messages from VCFRONT missing. Invalid communication from RCM RAM/ROM failure on startup, NVM corruption and ECC failure. (ECC Failure DTC, memory failures, checksum failure for appication) Requires a 12v reset, and the fault condition to be cleared Park assist features are unavailable PCS DCDC LV bus voltage reading differs from VCFRONT LV bus voltage reading by an excessive amount DCDC converter low voltage bus voltage sensor mismatches front vehicle controller's sensed value on CAN for more than three seconds DCDC converter low voltage bus voltage sensor matches front vehicle controller's sensed value on CAN for more than 500ms Inability to precharge HV bus, inability to support 12V system, inability to drive PCS DCDC timed out attempting to precharge HV bus DCDC converter timed out when attempting to precharge HV bus DCDC converter state machine clears faults Charger's input voltage sensor cannot lock the AC input waveform when commanded to start AC charging Charger phase state machine clears faults for phase Reduced AC charge rate or inability to AC charge PCS DCDC detects pack voltage is higher than the safety threshold to continue its operation DCDC converter detects that high voltage bus voltage exceeds overvoltage threshold

originalDescription: The radar sensor is cleared of any blockage. Radar internal object Tracking Self Test Not Done - This indicates that the radar has not seen any objects after being powered on. This alert is set on startup. A condition prevents successful CANRX (CAN message received) for the vehicle state message, relating to a checksum, a counter, or an out-of-bounds signal value. The vehicle state message sent from the driver assistance system (DAS) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) to the radar controller (RADC) ECU is not as expected / as required for the radar to be enabled. ECU Internal Critical Temperature Sensor Hardware Interference - The extrapolated sensor range is below the required range threshold. Most likely by increased noise from another HF (high frequency) source When the sensor detects interference from a HF source As soon as the noise from the HF source has subsided. Radar object detection is affected, radar might provide incorrect information for objects which will impact autopilot functionality. Redundant Temperature Sensor Implausible DAS_carConfig message was not received
title: RADC_a033 - sensor Never Aligned
description: Tesla alert code in the Radar Controller (RADC) module. The radar sensor is cleared of any blockage. Radar internal object Tracking Self Test Not Done - This indicates that the radar has not seen any objects after being powered on. This alert is set on startup. A condition prevents successful CANRX (CAN message received) for the vehicle state message, relating to a checksum, a counter, or an out-of-bounds signal value. The vehicle state message sent from the driver assistance system (DAS) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) to the radar controller (RADC) ECU is not as expected / as required for the radar to be enabled. ECU Internal Critical Temperature Sensor Hardware Interference - The extrapolated sensor range is below the required range threshold. Most likely by increased noise from another HF (high frequency) source When the sensor detects interference from a HF source As soon as the noise from the HF source has subsided. Radar object detection is affected, radar might provide incorrect information for objects which will impact autopilot functionality. Redundant Temperature Sensor Implausible DAS_carConfig message was not received

originalDescription: A condition prevents successful CANRX (CAN message received) for the steering wheel angle message, relating to a checksum, a counter, or an out-of-bounds signal value. The steering wheel angle message sent from the driver assistance system (DAS) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) to the radar controller (RADC) ECU is not as expected / as required for the radar to be enabled. ECU Internal fault - Hardware Watchdog ECU Internal fault - ADC Conversion Timeout ECU Internal fault - Mode Control Entry Error Vehicle Dynamics Error - the vehicle dynamics signals are out of spec, MIA or SNA ECU Internal ADC Overvoltage - Indicates an over voltage condition for the following voltage rails 1V25, 3V3, 4V5, 5V. Set when the voltages for the respective rails are above threshold. Cleared when over voltages return to nominal. Sensor function will be impaired, radar functions will be unavailable ECU Internal fault - ECU Module Configuration Error Received Country Does Not Match Learned Parameter When the learned parameters meet the ones being sent to the radar on CAN.

originalDescription: Steering Angle Sensor Speed Threshold Exceeded Supercharger timed-out during the voltage matching part of the sequence Voltage matching took too long to occur and the Supercharger timed out Voltage matching is successful and vehicle closes FC contactors Vehicle will not close FC contactors and charging will not start up. Supercharger measuered a low isolation measurement while connected to the vehicle. Supercharger isolation measurement result was less than the fault threshold Supercharger isolation measurement result is greater than the fault threshold Breakdown of isolation on the Supercharger or Vehicle's HV link. Supercharger did not receive all of the expected messages from the BMS. All BMS messages where received by the Supercharger. An emergency shutdown can occur while charging, or charging may never start up. Could not determine the status of the Proximity connection between the vehicle and the Supercharger Proximity Voltage is not within any valid range while connected to a Supercharger. Proximity returns to a valid value. Supercharger detected a voltage arc, either legitimately, or as a result of the HV circuit being opened under load. Supercharger detects a rapid change in energy on the HV bus, matching the signiture for an arc Supercharger has shutdown or been disconnected. May result in thermal damage to components, or welding/fatigue on FC contactors.

description: Tesla alert code in the Thermal Architecture System v2 (TAS2) module. Tesla Air Suspension (TAS) has received a message from the Gateway indicating that the vehicle experienced a crash event (SDM3 message for Model S and the RCM_collision message for Model X). TAS enters Limp Home Mode 40 to prevent any further leveling. For more information on Limp Home Mode 40, see Article #26133.

originalDescription: The alert checks for height sensor PWM faults every 10ms. A weighted counter increments by 10 for every repeated fault and triggers the fault at a threshold of 50. The weighted counter decrements by 1 for every valid reading and clears the fault when it reaches 0. TAS2_a030_PWMerrorFL will trigger if any of the following faults are set: PWM_OVER_MAX_DUTY Set:The height sensor PWM signal has had a duty cycle greater than 87.5% for 50ms.Clear:The height sensor PWM signal has had a duty cycle less than 87.5% for 500ms. PWM_UNDER_MIN_DUTY Set: The height sensor PWM signal has had a duty cycle less than 12.5% for 50ms.Clear:The height sensor PWM signal has had a duty cycle greater than 12.5% for 500ms. PWM_OVER_MAX_PERIOD Set: The height sensor PWM signal has had a period greater than 0.00131579 seconds for 50ms.Clear:The height sensor PWM signal has had a period less than 0.00131579 seconds for 500ms. PWM_UNDER_MIN_PERIOD Set: The height sensor PWM signal has had a period less than 0.00119048 seconds for 50ms.Clear:The height sensor PWM signal has had a period greater than 0.00119048 seconds for 500ms. PWM_OVER_MAX_DELTA_DUTY (cannot be set if transitioning to/from 0 or 100% duty cycle) Set: The height sensor PWM duty cycle has changed by more than 33% (3.3 * 10ms alert period) since its previous cycle for 50ms.Clear: The height sensor PWM duty cycle has changed by less than 33% (3.3 * 10ms alert period) since its previous cycle for 500ms.

originalDescription: The alert checks for height sensor PWM faults every 10ms. A weighted counter increments by 10 for every faulted reading and triggers the fault at a threshold of 50. Set: The front left (FL) height sensor PWM signal has had a duty cycle equal to 0% for 50ms. A weighted counter increments by 10 for every FAULT_TYPE_PWM_SHORT_CIRCUIT and triggers the fault at a threshold of 50. Clear: The front left (FL) height sensor PWM signal has had a duty cycle greater than 0% for 500ms. A weighted counter decrements (from threshold of 50) by 1 for every valid reading and clears the fault when it reaches 0.

originalDescription: The on-board 5V supply on the Tesla Air Suspension (TAS) board is sensed to be shorted to ground. The alert checks for 5VS0 power supply faults every 100ms. If this is a persistent warning, there is likely an issue with the board.

originalDescription: The Tesla Air Suspension Controller (TAS) supplies the system pressure sensor (located in the valve block) with 5V, and observes the analog pressure signal voltage to evaluate the pressure. In normal operation of the pressure sensor, the signal should range from approximately 10-90% of the supply voltage. Set: The gallery pressure sensor signal is below 4% the supply voltage (nominally 5V). voltage < 0.2 V Clear: The gallery pressure sensor signal is above 4% the supply voltage (nominally 5V). voltage > 0.2 V To convert from percent to bar: p(bar) = 23.7248 * p(percent) - 1.375
title: TAS2_a077 - pressure Sig V Too Low

originalDescription: The front left compression damping valve circuit is open, shorted, or returns an unexpected resistance. The damper valve is located on the damper body in the wheel well. Compression is the top valve and rebound is the bottom valve. The front damper valves are connected via pigtail harness from the front harness. The damper valves are driven by the Tesla Air Suspension ECU. Set: An open circuit, short circuit, or unexpected resistance value is detected on the valve after startup, or if the damper current deviates from the commanded current over time (indicating an unexpected circuit behavior). Clear: Drive rail cycle or ECU power cycle.

originalDescription: This alert is set each time the Tesla Air Suspension (TAS) board is set to Jack mode or wakes while in Jack mode (Chassis bus turns on).
title: TAS2_a200 - jack Mode Active
description: Tesla alert code in the Thermal Architecture System v2 (TAS2) module. This alert is set each time the Tesla Air Suspension (TAS) board is set to Jack mode or wakes while in Jack mode (Chassis bus turns on).

originalDescription: This alert is a load-shed request from Gateway (GTW) due to the voltage of the LV bus. Set: This alert is set when the GTW sends the CH_hvBatteryLow signal, which is a carry-over signal from Electronic Air Suspension (EAS). Despite the name of the alert, this message indicates to the air suspension controller that the LV system (not necessarily the LV battery or HV battery) is at a low voltage, and so it sends the controller into a limp mode to reduce the electrical loading on the LV system. It is likely to see this during a load-shed event where the LV system is not being properly supported by the DCDC. Clear: Clearing of the low high-voltage battery signal (CH_hvBatteryLow).
title: TAS2_a201 - low HV Battery
description: Tesla alert code in the Thermal Architecture System v2 (TAS2) module. This alert is a load-shed request from Gateway (GTW) due to the voltage of the LV bus. Set: This alert is set when the GTW sends the CH_hvBatteryLow signal, which is a carry-over signal from Electronic Air Suspension (EAS). Despite the name of the alert, this message indicates to the air suspension controller that the LV system (not necessarily the LV battery or HV battery) is at a low voltage, and so it sends the controller into a limp mode to reduce the electrical loading on the LV system. It is likely to see this during a load-shed event where the LV system is not being properly supported by the DCDC. Clear: Clearing of the low high-voltage battery signal (CH_hvBatteryLow).

originalDescription: This alert indicates that the ride height sensor positions are in an unexpected range compared to a known default value, which is determined in firmware. It is meant to catch gross miscalibrations. This alert and yellow warning lamp indicate that the calibration on at least one corner is between 25 and 50mm away from the default. If (during ride height calibration) the operator enters a height measurement that results in an offset that is 25-50mm from the default offset, the calibration will be accepted but will cause the aforementioned alert to be set, with a yellow warning lamp. Operation will not be restricted.If the operator enters a height measurement that results in an offset that is more than +/-50mm from the default offset, the Unified Diagnostic Services (UDS) routine will fail and nothing will change. No alert will appear and no calibration information will be saved.Each vehicle will have the ride height sensor in a slightly different position due to tolerance stackup. This is why Tesla Air Suspension (TAS) allows for a +/-25mm window before setting the alert.

originalDescription: The alert checks for exhaust gallery faults every 100ms. Set: After an exhaust procedure, gallery pressure stays above 5 Bar. Clear: After an exhaust procedure, gallery pressure decreases below 5 Bar. Note: An exhaust procedure is considered completed if one of the following is true: After opening the exhaust valve, gallery pressure has been constant within +/- 0.05 Bar for 50ms.The exhaust valve has been open for more than 2.5 seconds.The gallery is the volume within the valve block that gets pressurized prior to filling the reservoir or an air spring. The gallery is where the pressure sensor measures pressure when other components open their valves to connect to the gallery. The gallery is exhausted to atmosphere after a filling or a pressure check operation to reduce stress on the system. This is done by closing all valves except the exhaust valve, allowing the gallery pressure to decrease. The decrease in gallery pressure is limited by an internal check valve in the compressor, which is rated above atmospheric pressure.

description: Tesla alert code in the Thermal Architecture System v2 (TAS2) module. The alert checks for exhaust gallery faults every 100ms. The gallery is where the pressure sensor measures pressure when other components open their valves to connect to the gallery. originalDescription: The alert checks for compressor prefill faults every 100ms. Set: Compressor prefill will timeout during a prefill operation if the pressure derivative remains under 1 bar/second for 10 seconds. Clear: Single drive-rail cycle Note: A prefill operation is performed before opening the valves to an air spring or the reservoir. Prefill works by activating the compressor with the spring and reservoir valves closed, so that the gallery pressure increases. This reduces the change in pressure upon opening of the valves, similar to the precharge sequence for the high-voltage battery or on-board chargers. Prefill will fail if: a valve is open or leaking (pressure escapes from the gallery) the compressor does not run compressed air escapes before the valve block

originalDescription: The alert checks for high gallery pressure faults every 100ms. Set: Gallery pressure is greater than 19 bar for 1 second and Tesla Air Suspension (TAS) is in one of the following states: TAS_state = Fill_reservoir_from_CompressorTAS_state = Fill_spring_reservoir_from_CompressorTAS_state = Fill_spring_from_CompressorTAS_state = Fill_spring_from_Boost Clear: Single drive-rail cycle This indicates that the gallery pressure (where the pressure sensor is located) is over 19 bar, and has stayed at over 19 bar for one second. originalDescription: This alert checks for twistSustainedAtSpeed faults every 100ms. Twist indicates that two diagonal corners are higher than the other two and the chassis is reporting a twisted state - as if one wheel was parked on a large block. At speed, twist should not be observed because no road surface can hold the vehicle in a twisted state while in motion. Twist is calculated using the four corner heights: front right (FR), front left (FL), rear right (RR), and rear left (RL). Twist = | (FL+RR)-(FR+RL) | Set: When vehicle speed is greater 10mph AND twist is greater than 20mm for 10 seconds. Clear: When vehicle speed is less than 10mph OR twist is less than 20mm.
title: TAS2_a290 - twist Sustained At Speed

Twist = | (FL+RR)-(FR+RL) | Set: When vehicle speed is greater 10mph AND twist is greater than 20mm for 10 seconds. Clear: When vehicle speed is less than 10mph OR twist is less than 20mm.

originalDescription: Tesla Air Suspension 2 (TAS2) uses a common PCBA/Hardware ID for Model S and Model X, but uses different software applications (.hex files / configs) between the two vehicle types. If this alert is active, the chassis type of the firmware on the controller does not match the chassis type received on a car area network (CAN). Damping control will enter a backup state and endstop protection will not be active. Set: GTW_chassisType does not match the internal chassisType of the firmware on the controller. Clear: GTW_chassisType matches the internal chassisType of the firmware on the controller.

originalDescription: Tesla Air Suspension 2 (TAS2) uses a VIN learn because the system learns the damper travel endstops to compensate for any differences between height calibrations and nonlinearity between vehicles. The VIN learned to the controller does not match the VIN received on a car area network (CAN). Learned endstops may be incorrect. Damping control will enter a backup state and endstop protection will not be active. Set: The VIN learned to the controller does not match the VIN received on CAN. Clear: A VIN is saved by performing ride height calibration. Note: If using the normal air suspension calibration panel in TBX2 clears TAS2_a297_vinMismatch, perform another calibration using the "Air Suspension Calibration (Advanced)" Panel.

originalDescription: High rate of XRUN messages detected in the kernel logs. Audio stack will reset if this occured within 1minute from waking (not booting) and if the vehicle is parked. XRUN events correspond overrun/underrun of the buffer and can happen when a device/stream is closed or interrupted (ie. hanging a phone call) or high system load. An occasional XRUN is not necessarly an issue, but a high rate (40+ in 1sec) will have customer-facing impact Audio XRUN storm. The watchdog found at least 40 lines like `XRUN: pcmC0D**:0` between two `gpmrb_machine: XRUN marker` lines which are written every second Audio stack will reboot. This alert may be a symptom of a frozen MCU and potential VOR eMMC Status Register has error bits set Periodic eMMC check or post-wake eMMC status has an error bit set

description: Tesla alert code in the User Interface (UI) module. High rate of XRUN messages detected in the kernel logs. originalDescription: I2C arbitration loss causing all audio devices on the I2C bus to no longer be accessible. It prevents rediscovering the microphones and premium amp and reconfiguring the base audio amplifiers. Audio Weaver will be restarted by the watchdog to recover devices and unlock I2C bus Audio A2B I2C lockup, may be caused by A2B SYNC clock stopped at a specific moment and caused I2C arbitration loss which is not correctly recovered from Audio devices on the I2C bus are no longer accessible. Phone calls may be impacted with the far end no longer hearing the person in the car. In premium cars the bass sound may disappear and the audio may sound tinny and strange. If the base audio needs to be reset for any reason then it will also fail and then we'll have complete loss of audio Driver door open when gear is D/R/N. Triggers a continuous chime Driver door open when gear is D/R/N Shift into Park or close the driver door Potential danger of open door whilst driving

A message the local ECU expects to receive from the VCSEATD ECU hasn't been received within the expected timeout or the drop rate is too high while the VCSEATD is CAN active. The ECU is considered CAN active when VCFRONT_chGoToSleep is 0. All messages the local ECU expects to receive from the VCSEATD ECU are being received or the VCSEATD ECU is CAN inactive. VCSEATD functionality may be missing. The battery voltage measurement, nominally 12 volts, was higher than expected. The vbat voltage measurement is higher than 18.5 volts. One or more of the expected CAN messages from CHG were not received. A message the local ECU expects to receive from the CHG ECU hasn't been received within the expected timeout or the drop rate is too high while the CHG is CAN active. The ECU is considered CAN active when VCFRONT_ptGoToSleep is 0. All messages the local ECU expects to receive from the CHG ECU are being received or the CHG ECU is CAN inactive. CHG functionality may be missing. Electronic Parking Brake release was interrupted. Release will be retried.
